innerpython — Python internals¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

The InnerPython class¶

class core.innerpython.InnerPython(object)¶

The InnerPython class encapsulates Python introspection mechanisms and internal functions. That way it is easier to adapt to language changes.

classmethod get_class(name)¶
Parameters:name (string) – the name of a type or a class

the get_class method attempts to find the class object for the specified class named name. The name can be specified fully, e.g. Core.Date. get_class will attempt to load the module if it was not imported. It also searched the builtin types.

classmethod in_globals(name)¶
Parameters:name (string) – name of the variable

searches the global variable called name and return its values if found or None otherwise.

classmethod load_module(name)¶
Parameters:name (string) – name of a module, can be fully specified (e.g. mod1.mod2.mod)

attempts to load the module describes in name. The module can be fully specified:

from semperpy.core.innerpython import InnerPython as internal

date = internal.load_module('Core.Date')
d = date.Date(20090101)
print d
2009-01-01
